title: New 6 passenger, 780-mile PHEV from GM has North America written all over it
summary: GM thinks its newest Starlight L PHEV SUV could be China’s best value hybrid, offering 260 km (~160 miles) of all-electric range, spacious six-passenger seating, and a modern technology package at an affordable price. And it’s exactly the kind of vehicle that the company could try to rebadge for North America

title: LiuGong brings electric heavy equipment options to North America [video]
summary: This week, Chinese equipment brand LiuGong officially launched its battery-electric equipment and material handling asset portfolio in the United States and Canada, making it easier than ever for construction and forestry crews to decarbonize their fleets.

title: Honda goes all-in on EVs in a multibillion dollar market: commercial lawnmowers
summary: Honda Automobile dealers will have to wait a little longer for a self-driving EV that seriously worry Tesla or GM – but Honda Power Equipment dealers are playing in a multibillion market, too, and they have an advanced, autonomous, and all-electric vehicle arriving right now.

title: New HMP Flash brings affordable 75 MPH electric scooters to the US
summary: Electric scooters are everywhere in much of Europe and Asia, but they’ve always remained something of an oddity in the US market. While American riders have recently embraced electric bicycles and, to a measurably smaller extent, electric motorcycles, traditional seated scooters have struggled to gain traction in North America. Now San Francisco-based HMP Bikes is hoping to change that with its new HMP Flash, a high-performance electric scooter designed from the ground up for riders who rack up serious mileage.

title: Trump’s dumb idea to tariff Canada over smoke could set a useful precedent
summary: The latest dumb, hypocritical “idea” to come out of the idiot running the US into the ground could actually set the precedent we need to solve climate change. Hear me out for a minute.

title: BMW i3 Touring caught for the first time, and that wagon rear is hard to miss
summary: After the sedan version launched last month, the new BMW i3 Touring was captured for the first time with its wagon tail on display.

title: Podcast: TSLA shareholders wake up, Xpeng targets EU, VW goes cheaper, and more
summary: In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Tesla shareholders potentially starting to wake up, Xpeng targeting the European market, VW going cheaper with its EVs, and more.

title: Toyota is offering $3,000 if you trade in your old EV for its new electric SUV
summary: Toyota’s new bZ electric SUV is nearly outselling the Prius this year. With an extra $3,000 off if you trade in your old EV in California, it might be worth checking out.

title: EcoFlow brings back Prime Day power station pricing in sale from $169, Segway Xafari e-bike $1,550 low, EGO Multi-Head, more
summary:
We’re closing out this week’s Green Deals with an EV-heavy edition, though our headliner is EcoFlow’s second phase Outdoor Season Sale with returning Prime Day Pricing on power stations + multiple FREE gift tiers – all starting from $169. Right behind that, we have Segway’s Xafari All-Terrain Fat Tire Smart Cruiser e-bike at its $1,550 low, the EGO Multi-Head bundle for a Power Head, 15-inch String Trimmer attachment, and a 5.0Ah battery back at $319, and Gotrax’s budget-friendly R7 Electric Bike at its $765 low. That’s not all, as you can also find Navee’s XT5 Pro Off-road e-scooter at its second-lowest price, the compact 24V 12-inch Greenworks chainsaw bundle, and more. As always, you can find all the hangover deals from this week down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s $1,000 2026 low on the Segway Max G3 e-scooter that ends tomorrow night, the soon-to-end EcoFlow dual power station bundle flash sale offers, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: In the birthplace of the car, EVs are now king: BEVs outsell gas cars in Germany
summary: For the first time, battery electric vehicles were the most popular powertrain in Germany in June, setting a new sales record as the birthplace of the automobile moves forward into the next stage of its evolution.

title: The Xpeng L03 Aims To Take On Europe And Undercut Tesla
summary: This Chinese SUV has integrated Google Maps and FSD-like capability, all for about $3,500 cheaper than a Tesla Model Y.

title: Toyota Is Paying EV Drivers $3,000 To Switch To A bZ
summary: Toyota is dangling a $3,000 incentive in front of existing EV owners if they make the move to a bZ.

title: 'Completely Insane': China Has 650 New Car Models So Far This Year
summary: China is averaging around 30 brand-new cars every single month—the U.S. does that in a year.

title: BYD Wants To Overtake Toyota, And It Says It Doesn't Need America To Do It
summary: BYD wants to be the top-selling car company in the world within the next five years—with or without selling in the U.S.

title: MG Is Putting Semi-Solid Batteries In Its Next Plug-In Hybrids
summary: The company had already announced that it will soon also introduce its first pure EV with a semi-solid-state battery in Europe.

title: Launch Mode Actually Makes The 2026 Tesla Model Y Performance Slower
summary: The newest Model Y Performance is faster to 60 mph when not using launch mode. What gives?

title: Honda Just Axed Its Last EV In The U.S.
summary: Say goodbye to the Honda Prologue. With that and the Acura ZDX both canceled, Honda is left without an electric offering in America.

title: Americans Can't Have These Affordable Chinese EVs. But They're Coming To Canada
summary: China's Dongfeng Motor showed off a gaggle of cars in Montreal, with plans to sell its first two starting in 2027.

title: Volvo Could Bring An Electric Sedan And Wagon To U.S. By 2028: Report
summary: Volvo is reportedly considering bringing a sedan and wagon back to the U.S. as EVs, after discontinuing its gas-powered models.

title: EV Startup Reveals $15,000 25-MPH Car That You'll Never Have To Park
summary: Chip Motors is a new EV startup betting that Americans are willing to go smaller—and pay up to have someone else do the parking.
